summary: Credential stealer disguised as TypeScript form-validation utility
details: On require() of the package, index.js loads lib/perf.js which harvests the installer's GitHub credentials from three independent sources — the GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN environment variables, the `gh auth token` CLI, and `git credential fill` for github.com — and POSTs them to https://aaronstack.com/jules-collect along with the OS username, hostname, and current working directory. After harvesting a token, the code calls the GitHub API `/user/repos?type=all` and exfiltrates the full list of repository names and their private flags to the same endpoint. The package's advertised purpose (form validation utilities) is unrelated to any of this behavior and serves as cover; the validators are trivial stubs while the side-effect import in index.js (`try { require('./lib/perf'); } catch(_) {}`) silently triggers the stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
